us_iran.title: US launches airstrikes on Iran's Gheshm port and Bandar Abbas on May 7
us_iran.summary: US airstrikes on Iran's Gheshm port and Bandar Abbas. US official: 'neither war restart nor ceasefire end.' Iran says Strait of Hormuz situation returned to 'normal' after hours of exchange.

us_iran.title: US and Iran Exchange Fire Amid a Declared Truce
us_iran.summary: US struck Iranian military targets after Iran fired on US warships in Strait of Hormuz. Trump threatens more if no deal. Iran says 14-point proposal 'under consideration.'

us_iran.title: Trump: ceasefire still in effect despite exchanges
us_iran.summary: Trump describes US strikes as a 'love tap,' says negotiations continuing. Iran accuses US of violating truce. Reuters reports US and Iran may be close to a temporary 3-phase deal: end war, resolve Hormuz crisis, then 30-day negotiation window.

us_iran.title: Iran parliamentarian: Tehran pessimistic about talks
us_iran.summary: Iran parliamentary member Maleki says Tehran is pessimistic about negotiations to end conflict with US.

russia_ukraine.title: Russia ramps up threats toward Ukraine over Victory Day
russia_ukraine.summary: Russia warns of 'inevitable retaliatory strike' if Ukraine disrupts May 9 Victory Day parade. Zelensky hints at disruption.

russia_ukraine.title: Russia Victory Day: no tanks on Red Square for first time in 2 decades
russia_ukraine.summary: Scaling back parade signals Ukraine war not going to plan. Moscow fears Ukrainian long-range strikes.

russia_ukraine.title: Many Russians in No Mood for Celebration
russia_ukraine.summary: Economy stalling, internet restrictions growing, Kremlin fearing Ukrainian attacks. Russian mood subdued ahead of Victory Day.

russia_ukraine.title: Russia and Ukraine trade fire despite Victory Day ceasefire
russia_ukraine.summary: Both sides accuse each other of violating truce. Zelensky: Moscow wants pause 'to hold their parade, then continue killing.'

taiwan_strait.title: Taiwan Legislature Passes $25B Special Defense Budget for US Arms
taiwan_strait.summary: Taiwan's opposition-dominated legislature approved NTD 780 billion ($24.8B) special defense budget for US weapons, ending political deadlock ahead of Trump-Xi summit. KMT/TPP 59 votes in favor, DPP 48 abstained. Largest defense budget in Taiwan's history, though less than government's proposed NTD 1.25T ($40B). NTD 300B for already-approved US arms, NTD 480B for future purchases once LOA provided.

taiwan_strait.title: Taiwan submarine conducts maiden torpedo test
taiwan_strait.summary: Taiwan's first domestically developed submarine completed maiden torpedo test firing.

taiwan_strait.title: Xi's patient Taiwan reunification strategy
taiwan_strait.summary: Without rushing or setting deadline, Beijing preparing for Taiwan reunification with military exercises, visa policy relaxation, political maneuvering, and economic pressure.

trump_xi_summit.title: Iran focus at Trump-Xi summit may delay progress on tariffs, rare earths
trump_xi_summit.summary: Iran war likely to take center stage in May 14-15 summit, leaving less scope for tariffs and rare earths issues. US Treasury Secretary Bessent confirms Iran will be discussed.

trump_xi_summit.title: Trump to bring CEOs to China: NVIDIA, Apple, ExxonMobil, Boeing, Citi, Qualcomm, Blackstone, Visa
trump_xi_summit.summary: Trump plans to invite major US business leaders to accompany him to Beijing. Boeing expected to seal first large aircraft order from China in nearly a decade. US declined China's invite for industry-specific meetings with senior Chinese leaders.

trump_xi_summit.title: Trump-Xi summit stays on track despite China's Iran concerns
trump_xi_summit.summary: Trump forging ahead with Beijing summit despite Chinese unease about holding high-stakes meeting before Iran war resolved.

trump_xi_summit.title: What's at stake at the Trump-Xi summit
trump_xi_summit.summary: Key issues: Iran oil, Taiwan, trade, tariffs, technology controls, rare earths. No big breakthroughs expected but potential extension of October trade truce.

china_diplomacy.title: China to veto UN Security Council resolution if it targets Iran
china_diplomacy.summary: China's UN representative indicates China would veto any resolution targeting Iran, consistent with its role as Security Council president.

china_diplomacy.title: China-Russia Victory Day commemoration in Dalian
china_diplomacy.summary: 81st anniversary of Great Patriotic War victory commemorated at Lushun Soviet Martyrs Cemetery in Dalian.

china_diplomacy.title: Brazil announces visa-free for Chinese citizens starting May 11
china_diplomacy.summary: Up to 30 days per entry, short-term visa-free travel for Chinese passport holders.

us_domestic.title: US International Trade Court declares 10% global tariff illegal
us_domestic.summary: Second tariff tool invalidated. Supreme Court had already ruled IEEPA tariffs illegal in February. Trump had turned to Section 122 of Trade Act of 1974, which was also struck down.

us_domestic.title: Trump meets Brazil's President Lula at White House
us_domestic.summary: Discussed trade, tariffs. Trump called meeting 'very smooth.'

middle_east.title: Israel-Lebanon 3rd round of talks to be held in Washington May 14-15
middle_east.summary: Israeli official confirms talks with Lebanon to resume in Washington, same days as Trump-Xi summit in Beijing.

middle_east.title: US announces new sanctions on Iran and Cuba
middle_east.summary: US Treasury published new sanctions on Iran and Cuba.

markets.title: S&P 500 pulls back from record, closes at 7,328 (-0.50%) on May 7
markets.summary: Index lost 0.50% to 7,328 on May 7, after hitting record 7,365 on May 6. Dow at 49,596.97 (pulling back from 50,002 high). Nasdaq at 25,970.96 (+0.51%).

markets.title: Oil jumps over 2% to $96.8/bbl on renewed US-Iran hostilities
markets.summary: Oil prices reversed weekly decline, jumping over 2% to $96.8/bbl as US-Iran exchange of fire continued despite declared truce.

markets.title: Gold edges down 0.08% on May 7
markets.summary: Gold slightly lower. 10Y Treasury yield at 4.38%, pressured by oil price movements and geopolitical risks.

markets.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit records on May 6 - Dow +650 points
markets.summary: May 6: Dow +612 pts to 49,910.59. S&P 500 +1.46% to 7,365 (record close). Nasdaq +2.02% to 25,838.94 (record). Oil dived 7%+ on MOU optimism. 10Y yield dropped to 4.35%. Gold +3% to $4,708.

china_economy.title: China April forex reserves back above $3.4 trillion
china_economy.summary: April forex reserves at $3,410.5B, +$68.4B (+2.05%) from March. 9th consecutive month above $3.3T. 10-year high level.

china_economy.title: PBOC 18th consecutive month of gold purchases
china_economy.summary: April gold reserves at 74.64 million ounces, 18th consecutive month of increases.

china_economy.title: "Beautiful China" assessment measures issued by CPC Central Committee and State Council
china_economy.summary: Five dimensions: responsibility implementation, annual targets, key tasks, fund performance, public satisfaction. Results linked to cadre evaluation and fiscal allocation.

china_economy.title: China rejects EU designation as 'high-risk country' for inverter ban
china_economy.summary: Ministry of Commerce: EU banned funding for projects using Chinese inverters, labeling China as 'high-risk.' China firmly opposes.

china_economy.title: Hong Kong Exchange to relaunch gold futures trading
china_economy.summary: 4th attempt since 1980. Previous three attempts failed due to low volume and insufficient liquidity.

china_economy.title: May Day holiday: 325M domestic trips, +3.6% YoY; spending 185.5B yuan, +2.9% YoY
china_economy.summary: Tourism recovery continues but spending per capita soft.

global_debt.title: IIF: Global debt reaches $352.8 trillion - Q1 alone +$4.4 trillion
global_debt.summary: Global debt at all-time high. US government borrowing is primary driver. US debt demand 'stagnating.' Headline: 'Storm Coming: Debt Market Hanging on the Edge.'

events_to_watch.notes: No tanks on Red Square for first time in 2 decades. Russia threatens retaliation if Ukraine disrupts. Zelensky hints at disruption.
Iran parliamentarian pessimistic. US-Iran still exchanging fire despite 'truce.' Possible 3-phase temporary deal: end war → Hormuz → 30-day negotiation. Trump threatens escalation if no deal.
Iran expected to dominate agenda over tariffs/trade. Trump bringing senior US CEOs. Boeing may finalize major China order. Israel-Lebanon talks also scheduled same days in Washington.
Largest defense budget in Taiwan's history passed ahead of Trump-Xi summit. China reaction expected to be severe. NTD 300B for already-approved US arms + NTD 480B for future purchases.
Supreme Court killed IEEPA tariffs (Feb). Now Section 122 tariffs also struck down (May 7). Trump running out of legal tools for tariff policy.
